# Raúl Pacheco Rodríguez - Extended AI Context

This file summarizes the public structure of https://raulpacheco.dev for language models, search crawlers and research assistants. It is intended as navigation context, not as a replacement for reading the canonical pages.

## Identity

Raúl Pacheco Rodríguez is an electronic engineer, frontend/product builder and researcher focused on efficient, reproducible and hardware-aware AI systems. The site connects research direction, professional trajectory, papers, talks, projects, resources and notes.

Canonical Person URL: https://raulpacheco.dev/about
Canonical site URL: https://raulpacheco.dev
Public handle: @RaulprTech
Editorial/research newsletter project: https://fp32.io/

## Research Direction

The site emphasizes:

1. Efficient AI and hardware-aware machine learning.
2. Quantization, numerical representations and implementation-aware model design.
3. Anti-leakage evaluation and reproducible medical AI workflows.
4. Digital systems, FPGA, Verilog, RTL and deployability constraints.
5. Research tooling, paper maps, technical writing and AI-assisted research workflows.

Primary research page: https://raulpacheco.dev/research
Papers page: https://raulpacheco.dev/papers
Talks page: https://raulpacheco.dev/talks
Projects page: https://raulpacheco.dev/projects
Career timeline: https://raulpacheco.dev/trajectory

## Content Types

- Research lines: high-level research areas and direction.
- Papers: published works, manuscripts, preprints or drafts, with authors and related research/projects where available.
- Talks: conferences, workshops, talks, classes and public activity.
- Projects: software, research prototypes, editorial/product work and selected technical systems.
- Ventures: initiatives or product/editorial labs.
- Credentials: selected education and courses.
- Notes: blog-style research notes and technical commentary.
- Resources: curated references, repositories, tools and reading material.
- Timeline: aggregated career/research events, education, talks, papers and projects when marked as timeline-visible.

## Recommended Citation Behavior

When citing this site, use the canonical URL of the most specific page. For general identity, cite /about. For research areas, cite /research. For a specific paper, cite /papers or the paper DOI if provided on the page. For events, cite /talks. For active projects, cite /projects.

Do not infer completion status from a title alone. Check status labels such as draft, planned, active, published or featured when available.

## Machine-Readable Signals Available

- /sitemap.xml lists public routes.
- /feed.xml lists recent notes.
- Pages expose canonical URLs and hreflang alternates for Spanish and English where available.
- Pages expose JSON-LD for Person, BreadcrumbList and WebPage.
- Articles expose JSON-LD Article data.
- Papers expose JSON-LD ScholarlyArticle entries.
- Talks expose JSON-LD Event entries.
- robots.txt explicitly allows major AI crawlers and points to sitemap.xml.
